MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Apr-11-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Melanie Jones for defendant and opposing party: Interoceasn Ugland Mangement Corp.
NO OTHER APPEARANCE. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: plaintiff's motion to quash and modify deposition subpoenas: granted
The Court orders that the subpoenas to Pacific Cardiovascular Medical Clinic and Mercy Doctor's Medical Group are quashed. The Court notes the previous offer by plaintiff to stipulate to resolve the matters in dispute. Accordingly, the Court orders that plaintiff is prohibited from asserting a claim for mental and emotional distress or injury over and above that usually associated with the type of conduct alleged in the complaint and offering expert testimony regarding emotional distress. Furthermore, the trier of fact shall be instructed that plaintiff did not seek medical treatment for the symptoms identified in his responses to interrogatories.

The Court orders that the subpoenas to Matson Navigation (2), Patriot Contract Services, Horizon Lines, and Safeway are modified to strike the phrase: "any and all medical records and workers compensation records, regardless of date."

The Court finds that defendant persisted over objection and without substantial justification in an attempt to obtain information outside the permissible scope of discovery. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1011.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
